funding.title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s No. 2 role
funding.rawTextSnippet: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time as the company eyes a possible IPO and races to catch Anthropic in the enterprise market.

funding.title: Elon Musk praises Mythos/Fable, promises not to ‘cut off’ Anthropic
funding.rawTextSnippet: Should Anthropic trust Elon Musk to host its models? With about $40 billion in revenue at stake, Musk insists that the company can.

dailyBrief: In today's AI landscape, significant developments are reshaping the business environment. Terry Tao highlights the emergence of modern coding agents that enhance both old and new applications, potentially streamlining software development processes. Meanwhile, India's Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) is ramping up its AI capabilities by planning to hire up to 8,900 AI deployment engineers and actively seeking acquisitions to bolster its technological edge. On the educational front, a backlash against AI has led to a ban on laptops and phones for law students, raising concerns about the integration of technology in academic settings. Additionally, innovative projects like Mindwalk and Runeward are pushing the boundaries of AI by enabling 3D codebase visualizations and implementing policy gates for sandboxing AI agents, respectively, which could influence how businesses manage AI deployment and compliance.
